user.title: Power Insight
user.description:
Power Insight tracks the cost, savings, CO₂ impact and power distribution of your home's energy mix.

Give this configuration a name to get started. After setup, use the **Add device** button to add your grid connection, PV systems, batteries and consumers — then open the integration's options to choose which sensors to create.

**See https://github.com/Hoffmann77/ha-power-insight for more information.**

data_description.name: A name for this energy-mix configuration, for example "Home".

preset.title: Choose sensors
preset.description: Pick a starting point for which sensors Power Insight creates. You can change this at any time in the integration's options.

data_description.preset:
**Minimal** — power distribution and cost savings only.
**Recommended** — adds distribution ratios, export compensation, and charging/power-source tracking.
**Extended** — also adds real-time electricity cost rate sensors.
**All** — also adds levelized cost sensors (requires lifetime values configured per device).

error.invalid_name: The name cannot be empty or invalid.

init.title: Options
init.description: Choose a preset to apply the same sensor selection to all your devices, or choose **Custom** to configure each device type individually.

data_description.preset:
**Minimal** — power distribution and cost savings only.
**Recommended** — adds distribution ratios, export compensation, and charging/power-source tracking.
**Extended** — also adds real-time electricity cost rate sensors.
**All** — also adds levelized cost sensors (requires lifetime values configured per device).
**Custom** — configure each device type individually on the following pages.

combined.title: Combined sensors
combined.description: Sensors that aggregate power and costs across your whole energy mix.

data_description.distribution_power: Real-time power flow in Watts for each device in your energy mix.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Each device's share of total power flow as a percentage.

data_description.cost_method: **None** — no cost sensors. **Standard** — uses the live grid price per kWh. **Levelized** — uses each device's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOE). **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Also create sensors that track a running cost total (persists across restarts).

data_description.savings_method: **None** — no savings sensors. **Standard** — compares against the live grid price. **Levelized** — compares against each device's LCOE. **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Also create sensors that track a running savings total (persists across restarts).

data_description.distribution_power: Real-time grid import and export power in Watts.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Grid import/export as a share of total home power flow.
data_description.distribution_shares: Import and export each as a share of total grid throughput.

data_description.export_compensation_rate: Monetary value earned from power exported to the grid, calculated in real time.
data_description.export_compensation_total: Running total of compensation earned from grid exports (persists across restarts).

data_description.cost_method: **None** — no cost sensors. **Standard** — uses the live grid import price per kWh. Grid cost is always standard (no levelized option for the grid).
data_description.accumulate_costs: Also create a sensor that tracks a running import cost total (persists across restarts).

data_description.distribution_power: Real-time production and self-consumption power in Watts.
data_description.distribution_ratios: PV output as a share of total home power flow.
data_description.distribution_shares: Self-consumed and exported power each as a share of total PV production.

data_description.export_compensation_rate: Monetary value earned from PV power exported to the grid, calculated in real time.
data_description.export_compensation_total: Running total of compensation earned from PV exports (persists across restarts).

data_description.cost_method: **None** — no cost sensors. **Standard** — uses the live grid price. **Levelized** — uses the system's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOE). **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Also create sensors that track a running cost total (persists across restarts).

data_description.savings_method: **None** — no savings sensors. **Standard** — compares against the live grid price. **Levelized** — compares against the system's LCOE. **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Also create sensors that track a running savings total (persists across restarts).

data_description.distribution_power: Real-time charge and discharge power in Watts.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Battery charge/discharge as a share of total home power flow.
data_description.distribution_shares: Charge and discharge each as a share of total battery throughput.
data_description.charging_source_shares: How much each source (grid, PV) contributed to charging the battery.

data_description.export_compensation_rate: Monetary value earned from battery power exported to the grid, calculated in real time.
data_description.export_compensation_total: Running total of compensation earned from battery exports (persists across restarts).

data_description.cost_method: **None** — no cost sensors. **Standard** — uses the live grid price. **Levelized** — uses the battery's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOS). **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Also create sensors that track a running cost total (persists across restarts).

data_description.savings_method: **None** — no savings sensors. **Standard** — compares against the live grid price. **Levelized** — compares against the battery's LCOS. **Both** — creates sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Also create sensors that track a running savings total (persists across restarts).

data_description.power_source_shares: How much of this consumer's power came from each source (grid, PV, battery).

data_description.cost_method: **None** — no cost sensors. **Standard** — uses the live grid price. **Levelized** — uses the device's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOE). **Both** — creates sensors for each method.

error.reconfigure_adapters_first: These devices are missing data required by your selection: {adapters_needing_reconfigure}. Reconfigure them, or turn those sensors off again.

user.title: Add Device
user.description: Choose the type of device you want to add to your energy mix.

configure.title: Configure {adapter_type}
configure.description: Set up your {adapter_type}. Which fields are required depends on the sensors you enabled in the integration's options — for example, cost sensors need a price entity and levelized sensors need lifetime values.

data_description.name: A unique name for this device.
data_description.power_entity: Sensor that reports this device's power in W, kW or MW. Grid: positive = import, negative = export. PV/battery: positive = producing/discharging, negative = consuming/charging. Use 'Invert power direction' if your sensor uses the opposite sign.
data_description.power_entity_inverted: Turn on if your sensor reports power with the opposite sign to the convention above.
data_description.grid_electricity_price_entity: Sensor or input number with the current grid import price per kWh, in your Home Assistant currency. Required when cost or savings sensors are enabled.
data_description.co2_intensity_entity: Sensor with the current grid CO₂ intensity in g/kWh. Required when CO₂ sensors are enabled.
data_description.exports_power: Turn on if this device can feed surplus power back into the grid.
data_description.export_compensation: Amount you are paid per kWh fed into the grid, in your currency. Required when cost-savings or export-compensation sensors are enabled.
data_description.battery_efficiency: Round-trip efficiency in percent — the share of stored energy you get back when discharging. Used to attribute charging losses.
data_description.charge_from_adapters: Select which sources (the grid and/or your PV systems) this battery charges from. Used to attribute its stored energy and charging cost to each source.
data_description.lifetime_production: Total energy (kWh) you expect this device to produce over its whole lifetime. Combined with lifetime cost to derive the levelized (per-kWh) cost. Required when any levelized sensor is enabled.
data_description.lifetime_cost: Total purchase and installation cost of this device over its lifetime, in your currency. Combined with lifetime production to derive the levelized (per-kWh) cost. Required when any levelized cost sensor is enabled.
data_description.co2_footprint: Total CO₂ emitted to manufacture and install this device (kg). Used to derive its levelized CO₂ intensity per kWh. Required when any levelized CO₂ sensor is enabled.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure {adapter_type}
reconfigure.description: Update this device. Changing the lifetime cost or production values applies a correction factor that retroactively rescales this device's already-recorded levelized values.

data_description.power_entity: Sensor that reports this device's power in W, kW or MW.
data_description.power_entity_inverted: Turn on if your sensor reports power with the opposite sign to the expected convention.
data_description.grid_electricity_price_entity: Sensor or input number with the current grid import price per kWh, in your Home Assistant currency. Required when cost or savings sensors are enabled.
data_description.co2_intensity_entity: Sensor with the current grid CO₂ intensity in g/kWh. Required when CO₂ sensors are enabled.
data_description.charge_from_adapters: Select which sources (the grid and/or your PV systems) this battery charges from.
data_description.lifetime_production: Total energy (kWh) you expect this device to produce over its whole lifetime. Changing this rescales the device's already-recorded levelized values.
data_description.lifetime_cost: Total purchase and installation cost of this device over its lifetime, in your currency. Changing this rescales the device's already-recorded levelized values.

error.invalid_name: The name cannot be empty or invalid.
error.name_not_unique: A device with this name already exists. Please choose a unique name.
error.invalid_power_entity: The selected entity does not exist or does not report power (W, kW, MW).
error.invalid_price_entity: The selected entity does not exist or is unavailable.
error.invalid_co2_entity: The selected entity does not exist or is unavailable.
error.required: This field is required with the sensors you currently have enabled.

abort.grid_already_configured: A grid connection is already configured for this integration. Only one grid connection is supported per configuration.

no_grid_configured.title: Add a grid connection to {entry_title}
no_grid_configured.description:
**{entry_title}** has no grid connection yet, so Power Insight cannot calculate any costs, savings or power distribution — every device in your energy mix is measured against the grid.

To fix this:
1. Open **Settings → Devices & services** and select the Power Insight integration.
2. Click **Add device** and choose **Grid connection**.
3. Select the sensor that reports your home's grid power (positive = import, negative = export).

This warning clears automatically once a grid connection is added.

reconfigure_battery_adapters.title: Battery {battery_name} needs reconfiguration
reconfigure_battery_adapters.description: A charging source was added or removed. Please reconfigure **{battery_name}** so the correct charging sources are applied. Open the integration, find the device, and use the **Reconfigure** option.
